location.office_time_required: within commuting distance of hub; specific office time not stated

level_signals: 8–10 years of experience in strategy and operations roles
Owns carrier network strategy and cross-functional stakeholder management including senior leadership

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: you should have 8–10 years of experience in strategy and operations roles (e.g., consulting, finance, logistics, supply chain, carrier operations)
Demonstrated success developing and executing strategies that drive scalable, high-quality operations.
Ability to identify operational inefficiencies and design scalable processes, systems, and operating models. You default to repeatable systems over one-off solutions.

Strong analytical judgment, with the ability to synthesize quantitative and qualitative insights into clear recommendations and decisions. Proficiency in SQL or analytics platforms (e.g., Looker, Sigma, Hex).
Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills, with the ability to influence across Product, Engineering, Operations, and senior leadership.
Comfort with ambiguity and speed. You've operated in high-growth environments where the playbook didn't exist yet, and you wrote it anyway.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Direct experience with logistics operations and multi-market shipping networks is a strong plus.

scope.surface: Logistics Strategy & Operations — shipping performance, reliability, and pricing across all Whatnot markets
scope.business_model: marketplace / livestream shopping platform (B2C and B2B seller-facing)
primary_metrics_inferred: Shipping disruption resolution speed and reliability
Carrier network coverage across categories and geographies
core_responsibilities: Help define the operating model and strategy to enable scalable, high-quality logistics across all of Whatnot's markets
Own carrier performance and pricing health, monitoring key metrics, managing rate structures, and driving accountability with external carrier partners
Lead operational response to shipping disruptions, building scalable processes and playbooks that ensure fast, reliable resolution for buyers and sellers
Inform the product and technology roadmap, translating operational pain points into prioritized requirements for Product and Engineering
Shape carrier network strategy, evaluating coverage gaps, managing vendor relationships, and recommending updates as Whatnot scales into new categories and geographies
Partner cross-functionally with BD, Finance, Product, and Operations as a strategic thought partner, providing data-driven recommendations to inform shipping decisions

green_flags: Role has clear ownership scope (carrier performance, pricing, disruption response, carrier network strategy)
Company is high-growth with strong brand signal (#1 Best Startup Employer in America by Forbes)
Generous benefits package including 16 weeks paid parental leave, 401k match, family planning lifetime benefit
Multiple hub city options (New York, San Francisco, Phoenix, Los Angeles) offering geographic flexibility
Explicitly values low ego and growth mindset in team culture
Monthly dogfooding budget signals genuine product immersion culture
red_flags: Visa sponsorship not mentioned — unclear for international applicants
Role requires commuting distance to hub but JD describes team as 'remote co-located' — may create ambiguity about actual in-office expectations
8–10 years experience range phrased as a should-have, but context and title suggest this is effectively a hard floor
